queue.summary: Found that db.js uses inline NESTED_LOOP_JOIN, completely ignoring the HashJoin in volcano.js. This is why JOINs are 2.8s. Fix: wire db.js _select to use HashJoin when equi-join conditions detected. The code exists, its just not connected.

queue.task: Analyze db.js join execution path. Map exactly where nested loop happens and design hash join integration.
queue.summary: Mapped join path: db.js _executeJoin lines 1623-1637 does nested loop with full heap scan per left row. Need to detect equi-join conditions from join.on AST, build hash map on right table join column, probe for each left row. O(n*m) -> O(n+m). Filter condition: join.on is a BinaryExpression with = operator and column refs from different tables.

queue.task: Analyze UPDATE/DELETE performance: 498ms and 1028ms for 1K rows. Where is the bottleneck?
queue.summary: UPDATE/DELETE bottleneck: individual WAL commit per row (200 rows = 200 txns). Also _valuesToRow called on all rows including non-matching. Fix: batch WAL into single txn, skip row conversion for non-matching.

queue.task: Read a real database paper or codebase: LevelDB/RocksDB source, or Andy Pavlo lecture on MVCC
queue.summary: Deep dive into PostgreSQL MVCC internals (interdb.jp Ch.5). Implemented PG-style snapshots (xmin:xmax:xip_list) — correctly handles out-of-order commits. Wrote study notes comparing PG vs HenryDB. 3 new tests for snapshot behavior. Key insight: PG tracks exact active txids at snapshot time, not just a timestamp.

queue.task: Evening curiosity: explore something interesting in HenryDB (query compilation, vectorized execution, or columnar storage)
queue.summary: Built vectorized execution engine (MonetDB/X100-inspired): ColumnBatch, 7 operators (Scan/Filter/Project/HashAgg/Sort/Limit/HashJoin), 4 arithmetic vector ops. 23 tests passing. Benchmarked at 4.6M rows/sec. Study notes: in JS, row-at-a-time wins for simple ops (no interface dispatch) but vectorized shines for complex pipelines and aggregation.

queue.task: Reflect on evening explorations. What was surprising?
queue.summary: Key surprises: 1) PG snapshot is xmin:xmax:xip_list not just a timestamp — fixed a real correctness bug in our MVCC. 2) Hint bits: reads can write (lazy caching of commit status on tuple header). 3) Vectorized doesnt help in JS (V8 JIT handles polymorphism well, 3x slower for simple ops). Lesson: read real implementations before building your own.

queue.task: Implement hint bits in MVCCManager — cache commit status on version entries for faster visibility checks
queue.summary: Implemented PostgreSQL-style hint bits on MVCCHeap version entries. xminCommitted/xmaxCommitted flags set lazily during visibility checks. Fast path _isVisibleToSnapshot() skips full isVisible() when hint set. Benchmark: 2.8x speedup on repeated scans (35.7ms → 12.5ms for 10K rows). 18/18 mvcc-stress tests.

queue.task: Add READ COMMITTED isolation level to TransactionalDatabase (new snapshot per statement, PG default)
queue.summary: Added READ COMMITTED isolation level. begin({isolationLevel}) option, refreshSnapshot() takes new snapshot per statement. Extracted _takeSnapshot() with proper xmax=_nextTx. 3 new tests: RC sees commits between statements, RR doesnt, sequential commit tracking. 21/21 mvcc-stress. PG default isolation level now supported.

queue.task: Wire vectorized execution into HenryDB query engine as alternative path for aggregate queries
queue.summary: Built vectorized-bridge.js: canVectorize(ast) detects GROUP BY queries, vectorizedGroupBy(rows,ast) converts row→column→VecHashAgg→result. 10 tests for detection + correctness. Benchmark shows V8 JIT makes row-at-a-time 2.8x faster for simple agg. TypedArrays/SIMD would flip this.

queue.summary: LSM-Tree already implemented (lsm-tree.js, lsm-compaction.js, lsm.js). 22 tests passing. Skipping to query compiler.

queue.task: Build a simple query compiler: translate SQL AST to a stack-based bytecode VM. Execute queries as bytecode instead of tree walking.
queue.summary: Built SQLite VDBE-inspired query bytecode VM: 32 opcodes (control/data/table/math/compare/aggregate/hash/output), QueryCompiler (SELECT/WHERE/scalar agg/GROUP BY), Program.toString() for debugging. 14 tests. VM is 75x slower than direct JS (expected for interpreter; real value is portability, optimization passes, and debugging).

queue.task: Build a query plan optimizer with cost estimation: compare nested loop vs hash join vs merge join costs

queue.task: Implement Write-Ahead Log recovery with ARIES protocol: analysis, redo, undo phases

queue.summary: Built Counting Bloom Filter: Bloom filter with deletion support via counter arrays. O(k) insert/delete/contains. 1.1M inserts/sec, 1.3M lookups/sec. FPR 4.79% (target 5%), ghost rate 0.00% after deleting 50K of 100K items. 11/11 tests.

queue.task: Build a simple columnar compression library: RLE, delta encoding, dictionary encoding, bit-packing

queue.task: Build a page-level B+tree with concurrent access (latch crabbing protocol)
